title: Díreach Ón gCroí
description: A 2019 recording by Fergus Bogue on accordion, accompanied by Gearóid McNamara on piano, Gerry McMahon on guitar, and select tracks featuring Mairéad Bogue on fiddle. This digital recording features traditional Irish music with all proceeds benefiting a school in South Africa.

title: Hurry the Jug
description: Released December 7, 2020, this collaboration features Leonard Barry on uilleann pipes and whistles, Declan Folan on fiddle, and Shane McGowan on guitars and bass. The 11-track album showcases a graceful collision of traditional and contemporary Irish music with a dynamic rhythm section.
trackList: Hurry the Jug / The Pullet wants the cock
The Garvogue / Graf Spey / The Hare's Paw
The Stone in the Field / Caher Rua
Waiting for a Call / Brogan's Fancy
Father Quinn's Favourite / The Humours of Rahey / The Shoemakers Fancy
The Maids of Holywell / Megan's Reel / Kitty in the Lane

title: The Liffey Banks
description: Originally recorded in August 1971 and reissued in 2000, this Claddagh Records release features Tommy Potts in his distinctive solo fiddle style. The 22-track collection showcases his mastery of traditional Irish reels, jigs, hornpipes, airs, and songs, recorded at An Tóchar in County Wicklow.
trackList: The Drunken Sailor set: Galway Bay & The Drunken Sailor (Hornpipes)
An Raibh Tú ag an gCarraig? (Song)
The Long Hills of Mourne / The Old Boush (Reel)
Garrett Barry's Favourite set: Garrett Barry's, The Gander at the Prattie Hole & The Rambling Pitchfork (Jigs)
The Parting Glass & The Bunch of Keys (Song & Reel)

title: The Blue Diddle
description: A collection of Irish traditional tunes released in 2000 by The Arts Council & Co. This compilation features various artists performing a diverse selection of traditional Irish music.

title: Fear an Bhata and Captain O'Kane (O'Carolan)
description: Paul Dooley's interpretation of traditional airs on wire-strung harp, featuring 'Fear an Bhata' and 'Captain O'Kane.' This recording showcases Dooley's mastery of the medieval harper style with metal-strung harp in 2015.

title: CEOL 2018
description: Curated by Eoghan McDermott for RTÉ 2FM and Conradh na Gaeilge in 2018, this compilation features Irish artists performing their songs in the Irish language. The album was distributed to all schools in Ireland with download codes.

title: The Martin Family & the McGovern Family
description: A recording from 2013 featuring the Martin Family and the McGovern Family performing Irish traditional music. This album captures the family tradition of Irish music in County Leitrim.

title: How's About Now?
description: Tiernan Courell's debut solo EP, released June 24, 2022, features the Royal Conservatoire of Scotland graduate on flute and whistle. The 6-track collection showcases traditional Irish dance music arrangements with Craig Baxter on bodhran, Ali Hutton on guitar, Rory Matheson on piano, and James Lindsay on bass.
trackList: Reels: The Steeplechase/The Eel In The Sink/Hasbullas Big Day Out!
Jigs: Father Spodo Komodo's Trip to Togo/The Day the Ice Age Ended/How's About Now?
Hop Jigs: The Boys Of Ballisodare/The Surround
Reels: Dave Normaway MacDonald's Wedding/Eanach Mhic Coilin/St. Kilda Wedding

title: A Selection of Music from Racket Records 1997-2022
description: Released October 23, 2022, this 25-year celebration compilation from Rackety Records features 16 tracks of traditional Irish music. The album showcases acclaimed musicians including John Carty on banjo and fiddle, Michael McGoldrick on flute, Matt Molloy on flute, and Brian Rooney on fiddle.
trackList: John Carty & Michael McGoldrick - Waverly / Ríl Máirtín Shéamuis
At the Racket - The Stack O' Barley
Brian Rooney - Paddy from Portlaw/The Whistler at the Wake
James Carty - Queen's Hornpipe / Down The Meadow
The Good Mixer - Dermot Byrne's / Tom Busby's / The Stolen Purse
Maggie Carty & John Carty - Down by the Salley Gardens
John Carty & Brian Rooney - Morris Scanlon's/The Wandering Minstrel
At the Racket - Maloney Puts his Name Above the Door
James Carty - Hiding Daylight in Dark Corners/Bill Hoare's
Matt Molloy & John Carty - County Tyrone/Jimmy McGettrick's/The Lady's Cup of Tea
Curly Sullivan - My Lovely Irish Rose
John & James Carty - Ode to Lad
John Carty - The Steam Packet/Kiss the Maid behind the Barrell
Seainin McDonagh & James Carty - Jim Donaghues / The Silver Spear

title: Lightweights & Gentlemen
description: Lau's debut album, recorded in December 2006 and originally released in 2007, features Kris Drever on guitar and vocals, Aidan O'Rourke on fiddle, and Martin Green on accordion. The 2012 remaster includes 11 live tracks from the Bongo Club Edinburgh, showcasing modern Scottish and Celtic folk traditions.

title: Ré Órga
description: Released June 12, 2023 on Raelach Records, this album features Geraldine Cotter on piano and reed organ performing 15 compositions blending Irish traditional music with jazz and classical influences. The recording brings together an ensemble of Scottish and Irish musicians recorded across 2018-2023.
trackList: An Gaoth Aniar Aneas and Patsy Geary's
The Blackberry Blossom and Miss Thornton

title: 3'oh
description: Released July 13, 2019, this Mayo-Sligo trio collaboration features David Munnelly on accordion, Joseph McNulty on fiddle, and Shane McGowan on guitar. The 10-track album showcases traditional Irish reels, jigs, and original compositions in a dynamic traditional setting.
trackList: The Bog Carrot - Mossey Banks - Swallows tail
Seanamhac Tube Station - Rakes Of Clonmel
Factory Smoke - Polly Put the Kettle on

title: The Whistle Blower
description: Released October 30, 2018, this innovative album and multimedia project features Cormac Breatnach on whistles and vocals, Daire Bracken on fiddle and electronic loops, and Martin Tourish on accordion. The work serves as an artistic response to the 1976 Sallins Mail Train Robbery, combining traditional music with contemporary production.
trackList: On Board the Cork and Dublin Travelling Post Office While Justice Sleeps
See No Evil, Hear No Evil, Speak No Evil
The Beginning and the End: Aftermath

title: Lovely Leitrim (12 Songs)
description: A compilation celebrating County Leitrim featuring 12 traditional Irish ballads and folk songs. The album includes the iconic 'Lovely Leitrim,' a #1 hit in Ireland from 1966 written by NYPD officer Philip Fitzpatrick, a native of Aughavas in County Leitrim, performed as a graceful waltz.
